hard.owner_title: Vice President Asia Pacific Japan & ME
hard.next_step: RFP submitted on time 30 Jun; initial evaluation (document review) early-to-mid July, final presentation for shortlisted vendors late July, vendor selection late July to early August; awaiting official timeline update from Nidec IT (1 Jul).
hard.ais_why: Customer actively evaluates AI as a formal RFP criterion (asked what AI elements exist and which modules use them) and requested AI success cases (tail-spend analytics, autonomous negotiation) to benchmark, but it reads as diligent multi-vendor RFP evaluation rather than excitement: AI is one criterion among pricing, China support and references on a $550K deal.
hard.competitor: Coupa (via KPMG Japan); two undisclosed finalists in the shortlist-of-3

north_star_verdict.headline: Zycus is one of the final 3 (shortlisted 6 Jun from a 4-vendor RFI field) on a $550k global-suite deal, RFP delivered on time 30 Jun, and engagement is genuinely live and two-way, EY was still exchanging pricing clarifications through 2 Jul. The one thing that can lose it is price: Nidec flagged Zycus as significantly higher than the field and cost is a stated top-3 decision criterion, with no quantified ROI case yet to defend the premium before the late-July / early-August shortlist-to-2 decision.
north_star_verdict.math: 173 days to close (close 2026-12-26 from today 2026-07-06). Created 26 Mar 2026; entered Formal Evaluation 6 Jun 2026, about 30 days in stage. Buyer-stated path: initial document review early-mid July, final presentation late July, vendor selection late July to early August, then contract negotiation. Forward movement is real and on pace: 4-vendor RFI (31 Mar) to 3-vendor shortlist (6 Jun), RFP delivered on the 30 Jun deadline, active pricing clarifications 2 Jul. No stage slip; close date last moved 13 Apr (pulled IN from 2027-03-26 to 2026-12-26). Pace is feasible if Zycus makes the shortlist-of-2 in early August and clears pricing and SI questions before contracting.
evidence: Stage Formal Evaluation, forecast Pipeline, $550k, close 2026-12-26, 173 days out (SF authoritative facts)
RFP submitted on time 30 Jun with full 8-product scope including Merlin Intake (SF Products__c; Next_Step__c 30 Jun; Task 30 Jun 'ZYCUS- [NIDEC] RFP - Full response')
Shortlisted to 3 vendors after a strong 6 Jun presentation to the global team across Japan, Poland, US and China; buyer looking to cut to 2 (Next_Step__c 6 Jun)
Engagement is LIVE and two-way as of 2 Jul: EY's Yuanyuan Feng and Carl exchanged multiple pricing-clarification emails, ending with Feng confirming 'I have received the updated file' (Tasks 2 Jul)

Pricing is the live risk: the buyer stated the proposed pricing 'differs significantly from other vendors' and cost is one of Nidec's stated top-3 selection criteria (RFI QA call, 20 Apr; RFI call, 26 Mar)

No economic buyer engaged: Izabela Smulska Kowalczyk (Global CPO) was connected and invited to Horizon on 6 Jun but has not joined any call or evaluation milestone; the only mapped decision-maker is Manabu Serizawa, Procurement Officer (SF Contact Role)
SI partner unresolved: KPMG declined to partner with Zycus and is aligned with Coupa in Japan (Next_Step__c 31 Mar); Zycus is exploring TCS but nothing is locked
Access is intermediated: EY (Haru Kawachi, Yuanyuan Feng) runs the evaluation and controls scheduling and pricing dialogue, so Zycus reaches Nidec's own decision-makers only through the advisor

metrics.narrative: Nidec has articulated the business problem clearly but has NOT quantified it. On the 26 Mar RFI call Moriyama (Nidec purchasing) described the driver as building a single global procurement platform starting with a China pilot (7 sites) and expanding to roughly 40-45 countries, saying 'we have to change the current situation' and naming his top-3 priorities as global platform standardization, user experience, and cost. The 8-product suite requested (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Certinal, iRisk, Merlin Intake per Products__c) signals broad operational pain, but across every milestone (RFI 26 Mar, Q&A 20 Apr, RFP 30 Jun) no buyer put a number on it: no savings target, headcount reduction, cycle-time goal, or ROI threshold. Because cost is a stated top-3 criterion and Zycus is priced above the field, quantifying value is the single highest-leverage metrics move before the early-August decision.
sources: Avoma RFI call 26 Mar (Moriyama, top-3 priorities)
Avoma RFI QA call 20 Apr (pricing significantly higher)
SF Products__c (8-product scope)

economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority is named but not engaged. Izabela Smulska Kowalczyk (Global CPO) was connected and invited to Horizon on 6 Jun (Next_Step__c) and is the most senior mapped budget owner, but she has not appeared on a single call or evaluation gate. The only Salesforce-mapped decision-maker is Manabu Serizawa (Procurement Officer, Decision Maker per Contact Role), who runs procedural evaluation, not the commercial. In practice the commercial conversation is controlled by EY's Yuanyuan Feng, who drove the 2 Jul pricing-structure clarifications, meaning Zycus is negotiating price through an advisor rather than with the economic buyer. Inference: economic authority sits with the Global CPO and Nidec Japan procurement leadership, gated by EY. Getting Izabela into a direct working session before the shortlist-to-2 decision is the move to convert a named EB into an engaged one.
sources: Next_Step__c 6 Jun (Izabela Smulska Kowalczyk connected)
SF Contact Role (Manabu Serizawa, Decision Maker)
Tasks 2 Jul (Yuanyuan Feng, EY, running pricing)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are explicit and buyer-stated. On 26 Mar Moriyama named three drivers verbatim: 'system identification for the global global platform', 'user experiences' (because 'if it is very complicated to use, no one can use'), and cost ('finally, it's cost, but it is not simple, the prices, but also how it works'). Layered on top are China-specific requirements surfaced on the 20 Apr Q&A: in-country / Chinese-speaking support (Carl confirmed staff in Singapore and Hong Kong but 'no one based in Shanghai'), network/VPN considerations for Chinese users, data migration approach, master-data management, and AI capability (a formal RFP question asked what AI elements exist and which modules use them). References with operational KPIs were also requested. All are confirmed from calls and the RFP, not inferred.
sources: Avoma RFI call 26 Mar (Moriyama top-3 criteria)
Avoma RFI QA call 20 Apr (China support, AI, migration, MDM)
AIS_Why__c (AI criterion in RFP)

decision_process.narrative: The process is a formal EY-advised RFP run for Nidec Japan. Sequence read from the call and activity trail: RFI (26 Mar) with a 4-vendor field, RFI Q&A clarification session (20 Apr) facilitated by EY, remote demo/presentation (28 May), a global-team presentation (6 Jun, Japan/Poland/US/China) that cut the field to 3, RFP submission (30 Jun), then buyer-stated initial document review (early-mid July), final presentation for shortlisted vendors (late July), and vendor selection (late July to early August). EY (Haru Kawachi coordinated scheduling; Yuanyuan Feng runs pricing) gates all interactions and translates for the Nidec committee. Nidec IT (Tadashi Watanabe, HO IS BPIG) handled the RFP materials exchange. The approval chain beyond procurement (who signs off the $550k) is not yet visible, which is the process gap to close.
sources: Avoma calls 26 Mar and 20 Apr
Next_Step__c 6 Jun and 1 Jul (timeline)
Tasks 19 Jun / 11 Jun (Watanabe, Nidec IT); Tasks Jul (Feng/EY)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet in view because the deal is pre-selection (Formal Evaluation), which is normal for this stage. What IS known: Carl has repeatedly stressed contracting flexibility, offering to work on Nidec's paper rather than Zycus's ('we can do contracting on your contracts, not ours', 26 Mar) and flexible payment terms (30/60/90 days) and an included e-signature tool (20 Apr). The China rollout implies a phased implementation (7-site pilot then ~40 sites) and an SI partner for delivery, which is unresolved (KPMG declined, TCS explored). What would create visibility: confirm whether Nidec Japan uses buyer-standard paper, who signs at the $550k level, and whether an InfoSec/compliance review (China data-residency, PIPL/GDPR, already referenced on 3 Jun email) will run in parallel once shortlisted.
sources: Avoma RFI call 26 Mar (Carl on flexible contracting)
Avoma RFI QA call 20 Apr (payment terms, e-sign)
Task 3 Jun (China regulatory / PIPL/GDPR)

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is a fragmented, manual, multi-region procurement estate that Nidec wants to unify into one modern global platform, starting in China then rolling out globally. Moriyama framed it on 26 Mar: 'we are trying to build our platform for procurement as group... first from China region, then roll out globally' and, tellingly, 'we have to change the current situation' while acknowledging Japanese firms tend to keep the status quo. Specific operational pain points surfaced across calls: user-experience risk in eProcurement ('if it is very complicated to use, no one can use'), historical-data migration and cleansing across many source systems, master-data management across ERPs, and in-China usability/support. Pain owner is Nidec Japan purchasing (Moriyama, Manabu Serizawa), advised by EY. The pain is confirmed and articulated but not yet dollarized.
sources: Avoma RFI call 26 Mar (Moriyama, global unification, change driver)
Avoma RFI QA call 20 Apr (UX, migration, MDM, China support)

champion.narrative: There is no champion yet. Manabu Serizawa (Procurement Officer, the only SF Contact Role, Decision Maker) is a diligent procedural evaluator, moving RFI/Q&A/RFP steps forward, but shows none of the champion behaviours: no competitive intelligence shared, no introduction to the CPO or budget owner, no advocacy for Zycus inside the committee. Moriyama's 26 Mar line that Zycus is 'one of the very important candidate' is warm but process-neutral, not advocacy. Izabela Smulska Kowalczyk (Global CPO) is the sponsor Zycus needs but is passive (connected 6 Jun, no participation). The advisor layer (EY) is neutral by mandate, not a Zycus coach. What is missing: a named internal advocate who will carry the Merlin/AI and value story into the committee and defend the price. Building one, most plausibly a Nidec IT or procurement leader engaged during the RFP, ranks above any commercial step.
sources: SF Contact Role (Manabu Serizawa, no advocacy behaviours)
Next_Step__c 6 Jun (Izabela passive)
Avoma RFI call 26 Mar (Moriyama process-neutral warmth)

competition.narrative: This is a live 3-way finalist race, down from a 4-vendor RFI field (Next_Step__c 6 Jun). The one named rival is Coupa: KPMG declined to partner with Zycus because it is 'going with Coupa Jpn' (Next_Step__c 31 Mar), giving a Coupa bid a Big-4 SI advantage on the China pilot. The other two finalists are not named in any evidence and must not be invented. The buyer has NOT stated a preference for any vendor; the only competitive signal against Zycus is price, Nidec called the proposal 'significantly higher' than the field. Our wedge is the AI story (embedded, non-acquired AI on an Anthropic Claude backend with proprietary procurement prompt layers, agentic autonomous-negotiation and tail-spend use cases per the 20 Apr call) plus global-rollout flexibility and 16 existing China customers. We win by landing quantified AI/ROI value to defend the premium and neutralising KPMG-Coupa with our own SI partner.
sources: Next_Step__c 31 Mar (KPMG going with Coupa Japan)
Next_Step__c 6 Jun (shortlist of 3)
Avoma RFI QA call 20 Apr (pricing significantly higher; embedded AI differentiation)

deal_movement.summary: Deal is live and progressing on the buyer's own timeline. In the last 30 days it advanced from a strong 6 Jun global-team presentation (cut to final 3) to an on-time RFP submission (30 Jun) and into active pricing clarifications with EY through 2 Jul. Last real two-way buyer touch was the 2 Jul pricing-file exchange with EY's Yuanyuan Feng. Awaiting Nidec IT's promised official timeline update (flagged 1 Jul).

items.change: Stage advanced Qualified to Formal Evaluation
Shortlisted to final 3 after strong global-team presentation (Japan, Poland, US, China)
RFP submitted on time with full 8-product scope
EY (Yuanyuan Feng) ran pricing-structure clarifications; revised China pilot license figure to USD $420k confirmed received

competitive_position.summary: Live 3-way finalist race (down from 4 at RFI on 6 Jun). The single strongest current threat is Coupa: KPMG declined Zycus and is 'going with Coupa Jpn' (Next_Step__c 31 Mar), so a Coupa bid carries a Big-4 SI advantage on the China pilot, and it is amplified by the buyer flagging Zycus pricing as significantly higher than the field (20 Apr). The two other finalists remain unidentified in the evidence and are NOT named here. No competitor has been buyer-preferred; the fight is on price and implementation credibility, not product. Zycus wins by defending the premium with a quantified AI/ROI value case and locking its own SI to neutralise KPMG-Coupa.

competitors.quote: KPMG have rejected our inquiry to partner as they are going with Coupa Jpn. We are investigating TCS.
competitors.how_we_win: Neutralise the KPMG SI edge by locking a Japan SI partner for the China pilot, and out-position on embedded, non-acquired AI (Merlin/agentic negotiation) that Coupa bolts on.

competitors.name: Undisclosed finalist (identity not named in evidence)
competitors.quote: strong presentation to global team and Zycus is 1 of 3 now. Looking to shortlist to 2.
competitors.how_we_win: Secure the final-presentation slot and land the global-standardization plus UX story that maps to Moriyama's stated top-3 criteria.

competitors.name: Pricing premium / cost pressure (not a vendor, the live competitive lever)
competitors.quote: the proposed pricing differs significantly from, like, other vendors
competitors.how_we_win: Deliver an extended discount tied to a quantified ROI/value case so cost stops being the reason to eliminate Zycus.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus aligns well on the buyer's stated priorities of global-platform standardization and (pending demo proof) user experience, and differentiates on embedded AI, but is exposed on cost, the third stated priority, and partially exposed on in-China physical support.

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers matter most now: defend the price with quantified value, break through the EY advisor gate to Nidec's own decision-makers, and neutralise the KPMG-Coupa SI advantage.
flags: Close the pricing-premium gap before initial evaluation: pair the extended discount with a quantified ROI/value case, cost is a stated top-3 criterion and Zycus is flagged as significantly higher than the field.
Break the single-thread through EY/Manabu: secure a direct working session with Izabela Smulska Kowalczyk (Global CPO) and engage Nidec IT (Tadashi Watanabe) to build an internal advocate.
Lock a Japan SI partner for the China pilot (TCS or alternative) to neutralise the KPMG-Coupa implementation advantage before the shortlist-to-2 decision.

champion_strength.summary: No champion developed. Manabu Serizawa (Decision Maker, Procurement Officer) is a procedural evaluator with no advocacy behaviours, and the Global CPO (Izabela Smulska Kowalczyk) who could sponsor is passive. What is missing is a named internal advocate who will carry the value/AI story into the committee and defend the price; building one, most plausibly via Nidec IT (Watanabe) or a procurement leader engaged during the RFP, is the highest-leverage relationship move before the early-August decision.
champion_strength.source: SF Contact Role (Manabu, no advocacy); Next_Step__c 6 Jun (Izabela passive)

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus has a strong, differentiated AI story (embedded, non-acquired AI on an Anthropic Claude backend with proprietary procurement prompt layers, agentic autonomous-negotiation and tail-spend use cases) and the buyer asked to 'understand better to compare', but the differentiation has not yet been proven with named success cases, so the advantage is currently narrated, not landed.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 6.0 Moderate is accurate: the buyer treats AI as one formal RFP criterion (asked which modules use AI, requested autonomous-negotiation and tail-spend success cases) rather than a driver of excitement. The calls confirm curiosity and comparison intent, not urgency, consistent with a diligent multi-vendor evaluation.

items.detail: Buyer flagged Zycus as significantly higher than the field; cost is a stated top-3 criterion and no quantified ROI case anchors the premium.
items.source: Avoma RFI QA call 20 Apr; Tasks 2 Jul (pricing clarifications)

items.detail: KPMG declined Zycus and aligned with Coupa Japan; Zycus SI for the China pilot unresolved (TCS explored, not locked).
Economic buyer (Global CPO) connected but not engaged; the only decision-maker relationship is a procurement officer, with no exec sponsor validating the $550k.
Access is intermediated by EY; Zycus reaches Nidec's own decision-makers only through the advisor, limiting competitive intelligence and champion-building.
items.source: Avoma RFI call 26 Mar; Tasks 2 Jul (EY runs pricing)

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and two-way (pricing exchange with EY on 2 Jul, RFP delivered on time 30 Jun), not cooling. Confidence in the read is tempered by advisor-gated access and no direct EB contact.
items: On-time RFP submission 30 Jun and active 2 Jul pricing clarifications indicate a live, engaged evaluation.
Buyer-shared timeline (selection late July to early August) gives a concrete forcing event.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is a final-3 vendor on a $550k global-suite RFP with strong functional fit and live two-way engagement through 2 Jul; the one thing between us and the shortlist-of-2 is defending a price the buyer has called significantly higher than the field, with no ROI case and no engaged economic buyer yet.

win_position.text: Zycus made the final 3 after a strong 6 Jun global-team presentation and delivered the RFP on time on 30 Jun with the full 8-product suite, so we are a credible, well-fitted finalist heading into the early-August cut.
Nidec flagged Zycus pricing as 'significantly higher than other vendors' (20 Apr) and cost is one of Moriyama's stated top-3 criteria, and we still have no quantified ROI case to defend the premium.

No economic buyer is engaged, Global CPO Izabela Smulska Kowalczyk was connected 6 Jun but has never joined a session, and there is no internal champion, so we are selling through an EY advisor gate with no one carrying us inside the committee.

deal_momentum.text: Real forward motion: shortlisted 4-to-3 on 6 Jun, RFP in on the 30 Jun deadline, and EY still exchanging pricing files on 2 Jul, a live, two-way cadence.
The buyer has named concrete dates (initial review early-mid July, final presentation late July, selection late July to early August), giving a clear forcing event.
The next real gate is the shortlist-to-2 decision the buyer controls, and we are still waiting on Nidec IT's promised official timeline update (flagged 1 Jul).

customer_commitment.text: Nidec ran a formal EY-advised RFP, invested in a global-team presentation across four regions, and called Zycus 'one of the very important candidate' (26 Mar), signalling genuine intent to buy.
Commitment is to the process, not to Zycus: no buyer has expressed a preference, the CPO stays passive, and the only mapped decision-maker is a procurement officer.

deal_risk.text: Coupa carries a KPMG SI advantage on the China pilot (KPMG declined Zycus, 31 Mar) while our own SI (TCS) is unlocked.
Price is the live elimination lever, and without a quantified value case the premium is the most likely reason Zycus fails to make the shortlist-of-2.

deal_progression.evidence: Shortlisted to 3, RFP on time, active pricing dialogue 2 Jul.

customer_preference.evidence: Positive and engaged (final 3, 'one of the very important candidate', good functional fit per RFI) but no decisive lean toward Zycus and an open pricing concern; cost is a stated top-3 criterion.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain articulated (global unification, China-first rollout, UX, manual processes) but no ROI, savings, headcount, or cycle-time number quantified by the buyer.

scope_change.to: $550k full 8-product suite (China pilot 7 sites plus ~40 sites)
scope_change.detail: Amount raised $500k to $550k on 29 Jun with the full 8-product scope (including Merlin Intake) retained through RFP; no module reduction, phasing is pilot-then-rollout, not a scope cut.

critical_signals.text: Nidec called Zycus significantly higher than the field and cost is a stated top-3 criterion, so the premium is the live elimination risk unless a quantified value case defends it.
Zycus delivered the RFP on time on 30 Jun and EY was still exchanging pricing clarifications on 2 Jul, so engagement is live and two-way heading into the cut.
Coupa is the structural threat because KPMG declined Zycus and is backing Coupa in Japan, giving a rival bid a Big-4 SI edge on the China pilot.
Global CPO Izabela Smulska Kowalczyk was connected on 6 Jun but has never joined a session, so the budget owner is mapped yet un-engaged.
